response.justification: Bogdan is a versatile full-stack and web engineer with a broad background in TypeScript, Node.js, and various web technologies, but he is fundamentally misaligned with this role's core requirements. The position mandates 8 years of C++ experience — the resume shows only a 6-month stint at National Instruments writing C/C++ components plus a bachelor thesis project, totaling well under a year of meaningful C++ work. Similarly, the role demands 5+ years building large-scale infrastructure and distributed systems in a cloud or compute context; his closest experience is a micro-services role at Dunnhumby handling high-throughput HTTP traffic, but this is neither cloud-platform-focused nor C++-based. There is no evidence of technical lead ownership, cloud architecture design, or the kind of infrastructure depth this role requires.
strengths: Demonstrated experience with high-throughput distributed systems at Dunnhumby (millions of HTTP requests/second with async I/O and low-latency design)
Strong testing and TDD culture advocacy, including organizing workshops, code katas, and a D-language assertion library — relevant to establishing engineering best practices
Broad polyglot background (DLang, TypeScript, Node.js, Swift, Java, C#) showing adaptability across domains and willingness to learn
concerns: C++ experience is critically insufficient — the role requires 8 years; the resume shows roughly 6 months of C/C++ at National Instruments and one thesis project, with no ongoing or recent usage

No cloud platform or infrastructure architecture experience at the required depth — AWS CDK usage at Optilyz for a direct-mail pricing service does not constitute production cloud architecture ownership

No formal technical leadership or staff-level mentoring role in cloud or DevOps contexts; workshops and code katas are positive but do not substitute for 2+ years of engineering leadership in a compute platform environment
